Standard Name/Number: IEC PAS 63621:2026 – Artificial intelligence enabled medical devices - Data management
Summary: Publicly available specification establishing a framework for managing the data lifecycle for AI-enabled medical devices, covering planning, acquisition, development, de-identification, dataset composition, annotation, quality improvement, verification, analysis, provisioning, and decommissioning.
Healthcare Implications: Provides developers and manufacturers of AI-enabled medical devices with concrete data-governance expectations that can support safer validation, better traceability, stronger bias mitigation, and more consistent documentation across the device lifecycle. Health systems using these tools can use it as a reference point for procurement, oversight, and internal governance.
Keyword Tags: Safety & Risk; Clinical Quality & Efficacy; Transparency & Governance; Privacy & Data
Stakeholder Tags: Developers & Vendors; Providers & Health Systems; Regulators & Government

Standard Name/Number: ANSI/CTA-2135 – Performance Verification and Validation for Predictive Health AI Solutions
Summary: Voluntary standard establishing methodology for pre-market verification and validation of predictive health AI solutions. The standard focuses on non-generative predictive AI and addresses data verification, accuracy and reliability measurement, explainability, usability, model management, external validation, and real-world operational validation before deployment.
Healthcare Implications: Provides developers and health technology vendors with a concrete validation framework for predictive health AI tools before they enter real-world healthcare use. Health systems, payers, and regulators can use the standard as a reference point when evaluating vendor evidence for accuracy, data quality, demographic representativeness, explainability, model drift planning, and real-world performance in clinical or administrative workflows.
Keyword Tags: Safety & Risk; Clinical Quality & Efficacy; Transparency & Governance; Equity & Bias
Stakeholder Tags: Developers & Vendors; Providers & Health Systems; Payers & Purchasers; Regulators & Government
